examples.title: External to Conceptual Mapping
examples.description: Demonstrates how a user query at external level is transformed to conceptual level
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows how a user query at the external level is transformed into a query at the conceptual level using attribute and predicate mappings defined in the data dictionary.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) where n is number of attributes

examples.title: Conceptual to Internal Mapping Example
examples.description: Demonstrates query optimization from conceptual to internal schema in Python
examples.explanation: This Python code demonstrates how a DBMS transforms a conceptual query into an internal physical execution plan by selecting appropriate storage structures and access methods based on indexes and table organization.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for index lookup, O(n²) for join ordering

examples.title: Schema Definition Structure
examples.description: C structures representing the three-schema architecture components
examples.explanation: This C program demonstrates how to implement the three-schema architecture using data structures. It shows InternalSchema (physical storage), ConceptualEntity (logical structure), and ExternalSchema (user views), along with mapping structures to transform data between levels.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for basic operations, O(n) for searching mappings
